channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Liam Conejo Ramos' family fights immigration judge's ruling to deport them to Ecuador
item.description: The family of a 5-year-old Minnesota boy and his father who were detained by U.S. immigration agents in January is now fighting an immigration judge's ruling that rejects their claim for asylum.
item.content:encoded: The family of a 5-year-old Minnesota boy and his father who were detained by U.S. immigration agents in January is now fighting an immigration judge's ruling that rejects their claim for asylum.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina

item.title: Why a small town in Tennessee is at the center of a renewed global arms race
item.description: A small Tennessee town hopes to stop the construction of a facility that has a federal contract to refine depleted uranium into a metallic form the government needs to build nuclear weapons.
item.content:encoded: A small Tennessee town hopes to stop the construction of a facility that has a federal contract to refine depleted uranium into a metallic form the government needs to build nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Pierce Gentry

item.title: Iran's cultural heritage sites are being damaged by American and Israeli strikes
item.description: The cost of the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to Iran's most famous cultural heritage sites.
item.content:encoded: The cost of the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to Iran's most famous cultural heritage sites.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Sweet Pill's new album, 'Still, There's a Glow,' was forged through personal struggle
item.description: Philadelphia Emo band Sweet Pill has a new album out, Still There's a Glow. Lead singer Zayna Yousseff breaks down how this album reflected her mental health journey.
item.content:encoded: Philadelphia Emo band Sweet Pill has a new album out, Still There's a Glow. Lead singer Zayna Yousseff breaks down how this album reflected her mental health journey.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Scott

item.title: Journalist Maria Hinojosa shares her interview with Dolores Huerta
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Latino USA's Maria Hinojosa about her interview with Dolores Huerta, who revealed this week that her United Farm Workers co-founder Cesar Chavez raped her.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Latino USA's Maria Hinojosa about her interview with Dolores Huerta, who revealed this week that her United Farm Workers co-founder Cesar Chavez raped her.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: How one city moved quickly to remove a Cesar Chavez statue
item.description: Across the west, local and state officials are moving to scrub Cesar Chavez's name and image from schools, streets, murals and holidays honoring the famed labor leader. In San Fernando, California they've already pulled down a statue of him.
item.content:encoded: Across the west, local and state officials are moving to scrub Cesar Chavez's name and image from schools, streets, murals and holidays honoring the famed labor leader. In San Fernando, California they've already pulled down a statue of him.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Iranian missile fire kills 4 Palestinians in the West Bank
item.description: An Iranian missile strike in the West Bank killed four Palestinian women preparing for the Muslim holiday of Eid, highlighting the deadly reach of the Iran–Israel conflict into unprotected civilian areas.
item.content:encoded: An Iranian missile strike in the West Bank killed four Palestinian women preparing for the Muslim holiday of Eid, highlighting the deadly reach of the Iran–Israel conflict into unprotected civilian areas.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: 'Everybody was wearing black.' How the Iranian diaspora is observing Nowruz amid war
item.description: Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.
item.content:encoded:
haft sin — a traditional table set for Nowruz featuring symbolic objects.'/>

Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.

(Image credit: Sarah Ventre)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre

item.title: Trump has struggled to rewrite the narrative on major issues this week
item.description: President Trump is demanding concessions from Democrats, his own party and partners across the globe.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is demanding concessions from Democrats, his own party and partners across the globe.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Christina Applegate talks about the joy she's found even in hard times
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Christina Applegate tells Rachel Martin about a period in her life she daydreams about.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Christina Applegate tells Rachel Martin about a period in her life she daydreams about.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Hutchison
